findings.evidence_quote: With TurboTax, you can even [file your taxes for free with Form 1040](https://blog.turbotax.intuit.com/tax-tips/ways-to-file-your-taxes-with-turbotax-for-free-45946/) if you’re among the 37% of filers who qualify. [Get started today.](https://turbotax.intuit.com/)
findings.location: body paragraph under 'How TurboTax simplifies filing' heading
findings.reasoning: The rule triggers because TurboTax Free is mentioned ('file your taxes for free with Form 1040 ... 37% of filers who qualify'). The rule requires that immediately underneath this mention, the full disclosure be present: '~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A)' with a link to the modal. Here, the page only states '37% of filers who qualify' inline in the sentence itself, with no eligibility limitation text (the Simple Form 1040 / no schedules exception clause) appearing right underneath or even nearby. There is no separate disclosure block following the mention, and no linked modal reference to the simple-tax-returns disclosure. The strongest counter-reading is that the '37% of filers who qualify' phrase itself could be seen as satisfying part of the disclosure requirement, and one could argue the linked article 'file your taxes for free with Form 1040' effectively serves as the modal-equivalent link. However, the rule requires the disclosure to be presented directly underneath the free-mention, not merely alluded to within the same sentence, and the required Simple Form 1040 qualification/schedules language is completely absent from this page. Therefore this fails axis A and axis B does not match the approved library text (no verbatim or equivalent language present), making it an unapproved_violation flag.

findings.evidence_quote: With TurboTax, you can even [file your taxes for free with Form 1040](https://blog.turbotax.intuit.com/tax-tips/ways-to-file-your-taxes-with-turbotax-for-free-45946/) if you’re among the 37% of filers who qualify.
findings.location: Body copy, section "How TurboTax simplifies filing"
findings.reasoning: Rule R-01 is triggered whenever TurboTax free is mentioned. This sentence clearly advertises the ability to "file your taxes for free" with TurboTax, which is a TurboTax free filing offer. Under R-01, when such a free offer is mentioned, the specific eligibility disclosure text must appear right underneath. Here, the sentence only states "if you’re among the 37% of filers who qualify" without the required "Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A)." No such disclosure appears immediately beneath or adjacent to this mention, nor elsewhere on the page in a way that could reasonably be considered "right underneath." Thus the requirement is not met (axis A false). The wording also does not match the approved D-01 disclosure language (axis B false). A possible counter-argument is that this is merely linking to another article about free filing and not explicitly naming "TurboTax Free" or "Free Edition." However, the rule covers any mention of TurboTax free filing, and the wording here is a direct statement that users can file taxes for free with TurboTax, so the trigger clearly applies.

findings.evidence_quote: With TurboTax, you can even file your taxes for free with Form 1040 if you’re among the 37% of filers who qualify.
findings.location: Main body, section "How TurboTax simplifies filing"
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because the page makes a TurboTax free filing claim. The rule requires that right underneath such a mention the disclosure include both the eligibility percentage and the Simple Form 1040-only limitation with the specific schedule exceptions. While this sentence includes the 37% eligibility, there is no accompanying Simple Form 1040-only/schedules-excepted disclosure immediately underneath or anywhere adjacent. Therefore Axis A is not compliant. The exact approved D-01 wording is not present, so Axis B is false. Strongest counter-reading: one might argue the ‘37% of filers who qualify’ language suffices; however, the rule explicitly requires the Simple Form 1040-only limitation and specified exceptions right underneath, which are missing.

Trigger met by a direct mention of TurboTax Free Edition. There is no disclosure right underneath containing the required Simple Form 1040-only limitation with schedule exceptions and the ~37% qualifier. Thus Axis A is not compliant. The approved D-01 wording is not present, so Axis B is false. Counter-reading that a footer nav label should be exempt is rejected because the rule applies whenever TurboTax free is mentioned on the page.
